1. ### Slava [Astor] [FROG],  _—_ 3/25/25, 10:13 AM
    
    Not long after the captain posts the daily shifts, he returns with a single correction (which Benedin hopes is imperceptible) to the shifts. It turns out that he's assigned to guard duty of a certain high value hostage during the night shift. (@Danny let me know if you'd like any checks here. Forgery is a Deception skill that usually takes a long time to execute, but I'm hoping a minor edit can be done quickly enough) If no one confronts him, Benedin will head towards the tavern to enjoy some drinks, and possibly get in contact with the rest of the crew. If they're not present, he'll pass a few Messages to Satinder. @Jon [Lucas] @Wes [Riven] @Darren [Pavel] @Tom [Lee] @Billy [Milo]

3. ### Slava [Astor] [FROG],  _—_ 3/25/25, 10:14 AM
    
    Benedin thinks it will be less suspicious to place himself in charge of Tamary Numesti rather than Lord Numesti. How many guards are on that shift total?

    @Slava [Astor] Please make a forge document check. [https://2e.aonprd.com/Actions.aspx?ID=466](https://2e.aonprd.com/Actions.aspx?ID=466 "https://2e.aonprd.com/Actions.aspx?ID=466")

Success You create convincing forgeries and gain 1 EP you can use only when presenting some form of paperwork.Failure You create unconvincing documents. You gain 1 EP that (unknown to you) grants no benefit when used.Critical Failure As a failu...
    
5. There are just two guards on that duty because the main protection is the defenses of the fort itself.
    
6. ### Slava [Astor] [FROG],  _—_ 3/26/25, 12:18 AM
    
    I thought it would be Forgery, but assuming Benedin knew it would be society, he would pick Society and Deception in the morning as his tome skills [https://2e.aonprd.com/Actions.aspx?ID=2402&Redirected=1](https://2e.aonprd.com/Actions.aspx?ID=2402&Redirected=1 "https://2e.aonprd.com/Actions.aspx?ID=2402&Redirected=1")

    You create a forged document, usually over the course of a day or a week. The GM rolls a secret DC 20 Society check. If you need to forge a specific person's handwriting, you need a sample of that person's handwriting. Otherwise, you need only to have seen a similar document, and you gain up to a +4 circumstance bonus to the check (the GM determ...
    
7. I don't think we have a dice bot here @Danny
    
8. ### Slava [Astor] [FROG],  _—_ 3/26/25, 12:29 AM
    
    Benedin should have +17 from being Master, then +5 or +0 (Cha for deception, int for society), then +2 from Regalia just for deception. So either+24 or +17

12. ### Jon [Lucas] _—_ 3/26/25, 2:38 PM
    
    questions: Ask about other resistance members Other ways into the keep? What is happening with the hill giants? Will the patrolling ones be replaced?
    
13. ### Darren [Pavel] _—_ 3/26/25, 2:39 PM
    
    Does the resistance know where the prisoners are kept? Do they have access to any resources (such as magic scrolls we can purchase, etc.)?
    
14. ### Darren [Pavel] _—_ 3/26/25, 3:08 PM
    
    Recording other things we talked about tonight:
    
    - At night, Pavel can search for secret entrances to the keep. Focus on the hillside first, and then inside the tree line second. Automatic roll of 36 on stealth rolls.
    
    - Pavel can climb the tower and look in all the windows, then report back on everything he sees and draw a map. (Requires use of 10 minutes of invisibility, plus his climb speed of 20 ft.)
    
15. ### Slava [Astor] [FROG],  _—_ 3/26/25, 3:08 PM
    
    Benedin had described the location of the cellar to Lucas so that should be known, and with the Sending he confirms that's where they are.
    
16. ### Darren [Pavel] _—_ 3/26/25, 3:08 PM
    
    Benedin wondered if there's something to discover at the temple. I kind of wonder the same thing.
    
17. ### Darren [Pavel] _—_ 3/26/25, 3:10 PM
    
    Oh, with regard to Pavel's stealth, I should mention that he automatically stealths against hearing and other senses as well as sight. So he's really quite undetectable to anyone who has a perception dc of 36 or less.
    
18. ### Jon [Lucas] _—_ 3/26/25, 3:10 PM
    
    In case I miss part of the next session, if he has any 5th level slots left after the nightly activities, Lucas is planning to cast Sending before resting for the night to check in with Benedin
    
19. ### Darren [Pavel] _—_ 3/27/25, 5:26 AM
    
    Huh. I just noticed for the first time that this thread is in the role-play channel instead of the tactics channel. I assume there's no way to move it?
    
20. ### Slava [Astor] [FROG],  _—_ 3/27/25, 2:16 PM
    
    Left alone in the dark and musty cellar prison, Benedin tries to take the measure of Lord Numesti, the only other living soul. His manacles jingle as paces in the tiny cell. **"How'd you get yourself caught anyhow? Too brave to run when the tide turned? No secret tunnel out of the castle to take?"** _He pauses for an answer._ **"Do you regret it?"**
    
21. Using spoilers for now since the party is separated
    
22. ### Danny _—_ 3/27/25, 2:36 PM
    
    |"My daughter was at stake, so running was simply not an option -- nothing brave about it." "And I am sure there is a secret passage out of Fort Drelev, given how paranoid Hannis is, but I'm equally sure that he hasn't told even his wife where it is. He is the most selfish man alive."|
    
23. ### Slava [Astor] [FROG],  _—_ 3/27/25, 2:47 PM
    
    _Benedin grunts in acknowledgement, filing the information away for a later sending._ **"I thought your daughter would held here too. The one that didn't escape to the aptly named Freehaven, at least."**
    
24. ### Danny _—_ 3/27/25, 3:09 PM
    
    |she and the other prisoners that King Irovetti demanded to appease Armag were taken by Armag. [His voice cracks with emotion], I fear the worst..|
    
25. ### Slava [Astor] [FROG],  _—_ 4/9/25, 10:27 AM
    
    Benedin sighs. "I'm sorry, that's difficult news to bear. Are there any allies that can do anything about it?"
